reviews.review_description: I can't teleport or walk and it's extremely extremely uncomfortable why would this be released and you can't even turn you can't even snap turn there's no options for that why you cannot walk you cannot turn why are the same settings on the same remotes where is if you pointing something you will teleport somewhere else and it's not even a real teleport it's just a straight line that sticks out from the remote someone needs to answer this how do you put a game together that's supposed to be good but you forgot to have snap turning or you forgot to properly add the the teleporting instead of a straight line sticking out of the remotes or you can't even walk if there's no setting there's no objects for that someone needs to answer to that because this is absolutely not correct

reviews.review_title: nice game but need of love
reviews.review_description:
Game with enormous potential. A few suggestions: the teleport movement is uncomfortable — can’t we choose to move by walking instead? I hope they release updates soon to make the game more like Stardew Valley but with better graphics. For now, 4 stars, but I hope to raise them.
Please add coop mode

Bought this game on sale for like £2. Its surprisingly quite good. I would say its more of an introduction to vr farming sims but its not bad. I wouldn't pay more than £5 for it because I just think it's too small but I like the gameplay loop.

I’d like to rate this app better but have no idea how to use the controllers. There are levers to pull and a book to read (which I can’t open) and absolutely no instructions as to how to use the controllers to play the game ?

developer_response.body:
Hi there! 

Press and hold the "Grip" button on the controller to pick up the book, it should open automatically as soon as you hold it in your hand. If the book has more pages, you can turn the pages with your other hand and the same grip button. With the same interaction you can grab the lever and pull it up and down. Also you can teleport with the "Trigger" button as you are directing the controller to the area you want to go to. 

Hope that helps, happy playing

reviews.review_description: It's all the repetitive tasks you can expect from harvest moon without any of the movement, freedom, storyline, or anything you'd expect. Hopefully they plan on a HUGE overhaul of this game.

I have been waiting for this game to make it to Meta platform for a while and am so glad it is here.  This is an under represented genre on the platform so it was much welcomed.  Honestly this game should go to the main store but I am glad it still here.
If harvest moon or stardew valley had a baby with job or vacation simulator you would end up with Across the valley.  It is very simple, relaxing and enjoyable.  You get the satisfaction of slowly building your farm up from scratch and it feels rewarding.
I will have a couple gameplay videos posted today.  
Check them out on YouTube 
A-P-Eboys

Awesome game, simple and addictive. What bugs me are the animal's names tough. Difficult to memorise (we made the farm a family business and actually talk about them). We would love to name them ourselves. 

Also that beautiful pond needs a purpose! Just chilling and watching animals (fish, dragon fly, butterflies, frogs, ...) at the pond would be awesome.  

Whereas... maybe catching fish for a pet cat... since it would be too silly to catch that mouse. (I might want to name the mouse as well, as I share my house with it.)

reviews.review_title: First game I can cast on tv for my kids
reviews.review_description: Most games on the Meta store are too violent for me to play in front of my littles. This cute little farming sim is perfect. They help me pet the chickens and sheep. They help me scoop the poops. It’s money well spent on this game for me. I don’t mind the teleport to get around, but I wouldn’t mind being able to walk around on my farm either.
Idk how to walk in the game
